architecture_summary: The paper proposes Swin UNETR, a 3D transformer-based architecture with a Swin Transformer encoder that directly uses 3D patches and a CNN-based decoder with skip connections. The encoder is self-supervised pre-trained with masked volume inpainting, 3D rotation prediction, and contrastive learning on 5,050 CT scans, then fine-tuned for segmentation.

novel_contributions: a novel self-supervised learning framework with tailored proxy tasks for pre-training on CT image datasets
a novel 3D transformer-based architecture, dubbed as Swin UNETR, consisting of an encoder that extracts feature representations at multiple resolutions and is utilized for pre-training
successful pre-training on a cohort of 5,050 publicly available CT images from various applications
state-of-the-art on the test leaderboards of both MSD and BTCV datasets

key_claims.claim: The paper introduces Swin UNETR, a new 3D transformer-based architecture with a hierarchical Swin Transformer encoder for self-supervised pre-training on CT images.
The proposed framework uses masked volume inpainting, contrastive learning, and 3D rotation prediction as proxy tasks for learning anatomical representations.
The model is successfully pre-trained on 5,050 publicly available CT images from various body organs.
The paper claims the model is currently the state-of-the-art on the public test leaderboards of both MSD and BTCV datasets.
Swin UNETR achieves the best performance on the BTCV leaderboard and is reported as state-of-the-art Dice of 0.908.
Swin UNETR achieves the best average Dice of 78.68% across all ten MSD tasks and the top ranking in the MSD leaderboard.
Using all three self-supervised objectives gives the best BTCV fine-tuning result in the ablation study, with Dice 84.72 and HD 20.03.
The paper reports that self-supervised pre-training can reduce annotation effort by at least 40% for BTCV.
